headline: A US export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 dominates the recommended channels this cycle.
body: Better Stack reports the US government issued an export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers effective immediately [6], with Cole Medin attributing the removal to export-control directives and claimed security vulnerabilities while comparing Fable 5 against Claude Opus 4.8 [3], and Cyril Imhof framing it as an EU access ban [5]. IBM Technology's Mixture of Experts podcast separately analyzes Fable 5's tier routing system and Apple's pivot away from on-device AI toward an NVIDIA cloud-compute partnership [8], while Imhof details Fable 5's guardrails, memory, and agentic payment integration versus Mythos 5 [9]. On the speed front, Better Stack covers two sub-second inference pushes: Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed clearing 1,000 tokens per second via selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [2], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma hitting comparable speeds on H100s by applying diffusion techniques to shift inference from memory-bound to compute-bound [4]. Better Stack also reviews CopilotKit for agent-native generative UI [7] and react-call for invoking React modals as async functions [0].
